metadata.title: Your Version — AI+1 series 3/4
metadata.color_semantics: claude fidelity: INK #3D3929 all text; SPARK #D97757 the one accent; callout ink #161511 on cream. Do not retint.
metadata.greeting_note: Swedish short form (HAI budget) — HAI takes only the shortest forms (Hi · Ola · Hej · Ciao); never spell out HumanitariansAI in the greeting (the footer chip does that). Wagwan n/a (Bear only).
metadata.purpose: CUSTOMER pitch, not investor pitch: sell custom workshops, custom courses, and $1 Kindle books from a nonprofit doing good, and send viewers to the Humanitarians AI YouTube.
metadata.note: COLD OPEN LAW: B00 = ClaudeComposerAsk. SPARK-LINE LAW: every inner beat carries a short serif spark line. HANDOFF LAW: 2nd-to-last beat is the composer with a paste-ready action, greeting 'Your turn.'. OUTRO LAW: restate the title, poster serif, terracotta period. House grammar: >=1 PREDICT beat; verdict page carries the phase gate; decisions.json + DECISIONS.md ship beside this sheet.

callout_items.title: the ask

callout_items.body:
open in any CLI.
rewrite, cut, add.
your name on it.

callout_items.title: predict
callout_items.body:
something stays out
of the zip.
what is it?

callout_items.title: the reveal
callout_items.body:
we sell the base.
the judgment
was always yours.

beats.narration_text: … this is Liam, in for Bear. Can you get a custom version of a Humanitarians AI course? You can — and the path ends with a zip you own the rights to transform into anything: a book under your name, a course at your institution, a workshop your own instructors deliver.

props.command: can I get a custom version of this course?

beats.narration_text: Step one is an email. You write to info at humanitarians dot AI, describe the course you want, your audience, and what custom means for your context. They quote a price. No subscription model, no platform fee, no SaaS seat — a quoted price for a deliverable.

props.heading: Step one is an email.
props.body: You write to info at humanitarians dot AI, describe the course you want, your audience, and what custom means for your context. They quote a price. No subscription model, no platform fee, no SaaS seat — a quoted price for a deliverable.

beats.narration_text: What ships in the zip: base videos from the canonical course, exam banks tested against the source material, and — the part that distinguishes this from most licensing deals — transform rights. Rights to make it your book, your course, your workshop. Your brand on the front.

props.heading: What ships in the zip: base videos from the canonical course, exam banks tested
props.body: Rights to make it your book, your course, your workshop. Your brand on the front.

beats.narration_text: The zip opens in any CLI — Claude Code, Codex, next year's tool. You rewrite examples for your industry, cut the modules your curriculum doesn't cover, add the cases your learners actually deal with. Their content is the starting point; your professional judgment shapes what it becomes.

props.heading: The zip opens in any CLI — Claude Code, Codex, next year's tool.
props.body: You rewrite examples for your industry, cut the modules your curriculum doesn't cover, add the cases your learners actually deal with. Their content is the starting point; your professional judgment shapes what it becomes.

beats.narration_text: Here's a predict beat. What's the one thing that is explicitly not in the zip? Think about what distinguishes a subject-matter expert from an organized set of materials — and what was never theirs to sell in the first place.

props.heading: Here's a predict beat.
props.body: What's the one thing that is explicitly not in the zip? Think about what distinguishes a subject-matter expert from an organized set of materials — and what was never theirs to sell in the first place.

beats.narration_text: The answer is judgment. Humanitarians AI sells the starting point — vetted videos, tested exams — but what to teach, what to cut, what your specific learners actually need: that was never theirs to sell. The zip makes you faster. It does not make you optional. The teacher stays the teacher.

props.heading: The answer is judgment.
props.body: Humanitarians AI sells the starting point — vetted videos, tested exams — but what to teach, what to cut, what your specific learners actually need: that was never theirs to sell. The zip makes you faster. It does not make you optional. The teacher stays the teacher.

beats.narration_text: One-page summary. Email with course and audience → quoted price. The zip contains base videos, exam banks, and transform rights. It opens in any CLI — rewrite, rebrand, reorder. No platform, no lock-in. Phase gate: they ship the base; you transform it; your learners get your version.

artifactLines: Email info@humanitarians.ai: the course + your audience → a quoted price. No subscription.
The zip: base videos · exam banks · transform rights — your book, your course, your workshop.
Opens in any CLI — rewrite, rebrand, reorder. No platform, no lock-in.
Phase gate: we ship the base · you transform it · your learners get your version.

beats.narration_text: Your turn. The handoff prompt drafts the actual email: names the course, describes your audience in two sentences, and asks what a custom version costs and exactly what ships in the zip. Paste it and send what comes out — the quote costs nothing.

props.command: Draft the email to info@humanitarians.ai: name the course I want, describe my audience in two sentences, and ask what a custom version costs and exactly what ships in the zip.

beats.narration_text: Your version. Last in this playlist: straight into Canvas — how the zip becomes a live course in minutes. Liam, in for Bear.
